text_blocks.text: Ginga's conjecture says that this equation is also
a sufficient condition for p to be prime. Another consequence of
Fermat's little theorem is the following: if p is a prime number
other than 2 and 5, 1/p is always a recurring decimal, whose
period is - 1 or a divisor of p - 1. The fraction 1/p
expressed likewise in base q (rather than base 10) has similar
effect, provided that p is not a prime factor of q. Wilson's
theorem says that an integer p > 1 is prime if and only if
the factorial (p - 1] + 1 is divisible by p. Moreover, an
integer n > 4 is composite if and only if (n - 1)l is divisible
